description: Add admin session-based authentication to protect all admin routes, implement 14-day exam cooldown, and send distinct qualified/not-qualified emails with 7-day follow-up scheduling for failed candidates.
steps: In app/__init__.py, add Starlette SessionMiddleware using settings.SECRET_KEY as the secret

In app/routers/auth.py, modify the POST /admin/login handler to set request.session['admin_authenticated'] = True on successful login. Add a GET /admin/logout route that clears the session and redirects to /admin/login.

Create a dependency function (or inline check) in app/routers/admin.py that checks request.session.get('admin_authenticated'). If not authenticated, redirect to /admin/login. Apply this to all admin routes: /admin/dashboard, /admin/candidates, /admin/questions, /admin/exam/{exam_id}.

In app/routers/exam.py, in the start_exam route (GET /exam/start/{candidate_id}), before creating a new exam, query for the most recent completed exam for this candidate: SELECT completed_at FROM exams WHERE candidate_id = ? AND completed_at IS NOT NULL ORDER BY completed_at DESC LIMIT 1. If that date is within the last 14 days, render a 'cooldown' template (or the exam_start template with an error message) showing the date they can next attempt (completed_at + 14 days). Do NOT create a new exam row if cooldown is active.

In app/services/email_service.py, replace the single send_result_email function with two distinct email paths based on the 'passed' parameter: (a) If passed=True, send a beautiful, warm, celebratory welcome/congratulation email with charming language welcoming them to the next stage at Career Lab Consulting. (b) If passed=False, send an appropriate rejection email with encouragement, noting that a follow-up will be carried out after 7 days regarding the next interview opportunity.

In app/routers/exam.py submit_exam, after the email is sent for a failed candidate, insert a row into the notifications table with type='follow_up_7day', status='scheduled', and a message indicating follow-up is due 7 days from now. Include a scheduled_at field if the column exists, or encode the follow-up date in the message.
Add a 'scheduled_at' column to the notifications table in app/database.py (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS so it is safe for existing DBs -- use ALTER TABLE approach or add column in schema).

Update tests/test_app.py: (a) The test_admin_login_success test should now verify that after login, accessing /admin/dashboard returns 200 (session cookie is maintained by TestClient). (b) Add a test that accessing /admin/dashboard WITHOUT login redirects to /admin/login. (c) Add a test for the cooldown: register a candidate, complete an exam for them, then try to start another exam and verify they get a cooldown message. (d) Ensure existing tests still pass -- the admin dashboard test may need the client to login first.
acceptance_criteria: All admin routes (/admin/dashboard, /admin/candidates, /admin/questions, /admin/exam/{id}) redirect to /admin/login when accessed without a valid session
After successful login via POST /admin/login, the session cookie allows access to admin routes
A candidate who completed an exam within the last 14 days cannot start a new exam and sees a message with the eligible retry date
When a candidate passes, the email sent is a warm, celebratory welcome message
When a candidate fails, the email sent mentions encouragement and states that follow-up will happen after 7 days
A notification record with type 'follow_up_7day' is created in the notifications table for failed candidates
All existing tests continue to pass (27 tests) plus new tests are added
Running python -m pytest tests/ shows all tests passing
